spec: RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme (JCS): canonical bytes and SHA-256 of the canonical form
provenance: Generated from the agent-passport-system canonicalizeJCS reference (npm 3.3.1) and verified by the suite runner's vendored RFC 8785 canonicalizer, proving byte-parity between the reference and the standalone runner.

vectors.name: float-tenth
vectors.description: 0.1 has no exact binary representation; ECMAScript Number::toString (RFC 8785) emits the shortest round-trip decimal.

vectors.name: float-1e21-boundary
vectors.description: 1e21 is the ECMAScript threshold at which Number::toString switches to exponential notation ("1e+21").

vectors.name: negative-zero
vectors.description: JSON cannot carry a distinct -0 (it parses to 0); JCS renders both as "0". This pins that both map to the same bytes.

vectors.name: integer-above-2pow53
vectors.description: 9007199254740994 (2^53 + 2) is exactly representable as an IEEE 754 double and serializes as a plain integer, not exponential.

vectors.name: small-exponent-vs-decimal
vectors.description: Per ECMAScript, 1e-7 stays exponential ("1e-7") while 1e-6 becomes decimal ("0.000001"). Object keys are also sorted (dec before exp).

vectors.name: astral-key-ordering
vectors.description: Keys sort by UTF-16 code unit, not code point: the astral-plane key U+1D306 (surrogate lead unit 0xD834) sorts BEFORE the BMP key U+FF61 (0xFF61), because 0xD834 < 0xFF61.

vectors.name: nfd-key-used-as-given
vectors.description: JCS sorts and emits object keys exactly as given and does NOT Unicode-normalize keys. This key is NFD (e + U+0301 combining acute) and is preserved as NFD, distinct from its NFC form.

vectors.name: nested-object-and-array
vectors.description: Nested object keys sort recursively; array element order is always preserved (arrays are never reordered).
